description: Study 3: forced-viz loop. 6 problems x 3 conditions (b = optional flat control; vizprimed = frictions removed + worked primer, still optional; forced = hard-required plot-then-Read loop) x 3 runs = 54 runs.
motivation: Separate the adoption lever (information/priming vs requirement) and test whether forced visualization changes the formalization outcome.
formal_results: viz usage: b 6%, vizprimed 11%, forced 100% (PNG read-back: b 0%, vizprimed 0%, forced 100%). solved*: b 6/18, vizprimed 5/18, forced 6/18 (forcing did not raise solve rate). viz_changed_approach=true in 0/18 of both vizprimed and forced runs.
results_description: Soft priming barely moves adoption (6->11%); only hard requirement reaches 100% use. Even when used and read, the plot is self-reported as not changing the approach; the closed form came from numeric spot-checks. The Read-the-PNG second step is never self-discovered.
conclusions: Adoption is governed by requirement, not information. Forced viz is confirmatory, not corrective: it ratifies an answer the model already had and does not improve solving on a true-claim corpus.
